description_long: This object is a traditional pickaxe with a long, straight wooden handle and a robust, double-sided metal head. The head features two pointed, slightly curved prongs extending horizontally in opposite directions, ideal for breaking hard surfaces. The wood of the handle appears dark and weathered, suggesting durability and a comfortable grip. The metal head is dark, possibly steel or iron, and shows a matte, slightly rough finish. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, and the construction is utilitarian. The overall design is simple, functional, and typical of hand tools used for digging or breaking up urban surfaces.
description: A classic pickaxe with a dark wooden handle and a double-pointed metal head, designed for breaking hard surfaces. The tool has a rugged, utilitarian appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the double-pointed metal head and the straight wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the pickaxe and the thickness of the metal head.
description_view_2: Rear view, similar to the front, displaying the opposite side of the metal head and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the alignment of the handle with the head.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle, cylindrical, dark finish, no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_blade_type: Double-pointed, pickaxe-style metal head with two curved, tapered prongs.
